extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds for all reachable executions. Analysis: (1) sent is set to true only by t_send_allowed, which is guarded by `not opted_out` — so sent can only become true when opted_out is false at that moment, satisfying the property (antecedent false). (2) opted_out is monotone: only t_optout writes it (to true), and no transition ever sets it back to false (t_send_allowed/t_send_blocked/t_tick leave opted_out unchanged). (3) t_optout sets opted_out:=true AND sent:=false simultaneously, so the state immediately after opting out has sent=false. (4) Once opted_out is true, t_send_allowed's guard (`not opted_out`) is permanently false, so sent can never be raised again; any send_attempt fires t_send_blocked (sent:=false), and both tick and optout also force sent:=false. Therefore every reachable state with opted_out=true has sent=false. The initial state (opted_out=false) satisfies the property vacuously. The property holds within 5 events and in general.
